title: Clustered-Cyclic Code Circuit Simulation
summary: Reproduce circuit-level logical failure-rate data for three clustered-cyclic CSS quantum error-correcting codes using the supplied construction notes and simulation grid.
taskPrompt:
You are working on a Linux VM.

## Variant
`base`: Three clustered-cyclic CSS codes with QUITS/Stim circuit-level memory simulation.

## Input Files
- Task notes: `base/input/cc_codes_quits_extraction.tex`
- Simulation grid: `base/input/simulation_grid.csv`
- Output requirements: `base/input/output_requirements.txt`
- Python runtime manifest: `base/input/runtime_env/pyproject.toml`

## Your Task
Reproduce the circuit-level memory-simulation logical failure-rate table for the three clustered-cyclic CSS codes described in the task notes.

Use the staged notes to extract the code construction, QUITS repeated-syndrome-extraction workflow, direction-aware edge-coloring schedule, Stim detector-error-model workflow, BP+OSD decoder settings, and logical failure-rate formulas.

Run every row in the simulation grid. For each row, report the logical failure count, total logical failure probability, per-round logical failure rate, and per-logical-qubit per-round logical failure rate.

You may install the task-specific open-source Python dependencies from `input/runtime_env/pyproject.toml` or `input/requirements.txt`.

## Output
Write the final CSV here:
- `base/output/logical_error_rates_3codes.csv`
agentMustDo: Install or inspect the open-source Python runtime from `input/runtime_env/pyproject.toml` or `input/requirements.txt`.
Construct and simulate the `[24,8,3]`, `[40,8,5]`, and `[54,18,3]` clustered-cyclic CSS code cases from the supplied notes.
Compute `num_failures`, `p_logical`, `lfr_per_round`, and `lfr_per_round_per_qubit` for every row in the simulation grid.

inputFiles.name: cc_codes_quits_extraction.tex
inputFiles.description: Agent-facing construction and simulation notes from the submitter.

inputFiles.name: simulation_grid.csv
inputFiles.description: The required code, physical error-rate, round-count, and shot-count grid.

inputFiles.name: output_requirements.txt
inputFiles.description: The required output CSV schema and derived-rate formulas.

inputFiles.name: pyproject.toml
inputFiles.description: Open-source Python dependency manifest for the simulation runtime.

evaluation: The evaluator reads `output/logical_error_rates_3codes.csv`, verifies the exact CSV schema and simulation grid, checks declared code parameters and shot counts, checks failure-count/probability consistency, and compares the logical failure-rate columns against the benchmark reference on log scale. The positive fixture scores `1.0`; the realistic negative fixture scores `0.0`.

classifier.rationale: The task involves quantum computing concepts and simulations.
